---
title: "Chad Hazlett"
pagetitle: "Chad Hazlett"
toc: false
page-layout: full
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <meta name="description" content="Chad Hazlett — Professor of Political Science and Statistics at UCLA. Causal inference methods for politics, policy, and medicine.">
---

```{=html}
<section class="hero">
  <div class="hero-inner">
    <img src="assets/headshot.jpg" alt="Chad Hazlett" class="hero-photo">
    <div class="hero-text">
      <h1>Chad Hazlett</h1>
      <p class="hero-title">Professor of Political Science and Statistics, UCLA</p>
      <p class="hero-strap">Causal inference methods for hard questions in politics, policy, and medicine.</p>
      <div class="hero-ctas">
        <a href="research.html">Research</a>
        <a href="publications.html">Publications</a>
        <a href="software.html">Software</a>
        <a href="teaching.html">Teaching</a>
        <a href="cv.html">CV</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="featured-section">
  <h2>Featured work</h2>
  <div id="featured-carousel" class="carousel slide" data-bs-ride="carousel" data-bs-interval="6000">
    <div class="carousel-indicators">
      <button type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide-to="0" class="active" aria-current="true" aria-label="Slide 1"></button>
      <button type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide-to="1" aria-label="Slide 2"></button>
      <button type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide-to="2" aria-label="Slide 3"></button>
      <button type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide-to="3" aria-label="Slide 4"></button>
      <button type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide-to="4" aria-label="Slide 5"></button>
      <button type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide-to="5" aria-label="Slide 6"></button>
      <button type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide-to="6" aria-label="Slide 7"></button>
    </div>
    <div class="carousel-inner">
      <div class="carousel-item active">
        <div class="featured-paper">
          <h3>Making sense of sensitivity: Extending omitted variable bias</h3>
          <p class="lead">A general, interpretable framework for asking <em>how much</em> unobserved confounding would have to exist to overturn a result — now the standard tool for honest observational-studies reporting.</p>
          <p class="meta">Cinelli &amp; Hazlett · <em>JRSS B</em> · 2020</p>
          <a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/rssb.12348" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Read →</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="carousel-item">
        <div class="featured-paper">
          <h3>kpop: A kernel balancing approach for survey weighting</h3>
          <p class="lead">Weights non-representative survey samples to match population targets in a reproducing-kernel space — reducing the specification assumptions practitioners usually have to defend.</p>
          <p class="meta">Hartman, Hazlett &amp; Sterbenz · <em>JRSS A</em> · 2024</p>
          <a href="https://doi.org/10.1093/jrsssa/qnae082" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Read →</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="carousel-item">
        <div class="featured-paper">
          <h3>Wildfire exposure increases pro-environment voting</h3>
          <p class="lead">A natural-experiment study of how climate-related hazards reshape political behavior — and where partisanship still dominates.</p>
          <p class="meta">Hazlett &amp; Mildenberger · <em>APSR</em> · 2020</p>
          <a href="https://doi.org/10.1017/S0003055420000441" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Read →</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="carousel-item">
        <div class="featured-paper">
          <h3>From "is it unconfounded?" to "how much confounding would it take?"</h3>
          <p class="lead">A sensitivity-based alternative to binary debates over causal identification in observational social science — applied to support for peace in Colombia.</p>
          <p class="meta">Hazlett &amp; Parente · <em>Journal of Politics</em> · 2023</p>
          <a href="https://doi.org/10.1086/723995" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Read →</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="carousel-item">
        <div class="featured-paper">
          <h3>Safe learning outside randomized trials: SCQE for COVID-19 therapies</h3>
          <p class="lead">Bounds the effects of remdesivir, hydroxychloroquine, and dexamethasone using only a plausible assumption about baseline trends — demonstrating how SCQE could have informed safe decisions before RCTs completed.</p>
          <p class="meta">Hazlett &amp; Wulf et al. · <em>Observational Studies</em> · 2025</p>
          <a href="https://doi.org/10.1353/obs.2025.a973069" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Read →</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="carousel-item">
        <div class="featured-paper">
          <h3>Seeing like a district: What close-election designs for leader characteristics can and cannot tell us</h3>
          <p class="lead">Clarifies what the popular close-election regression discontinuity can — and cannot — identify when researchers use it to study how leader traits shape policy and conflict.</p>
          <p class="meta">Bertoli &amp; Hazlett · <em>Political Analysis</em> · 2025</p>
          <a href="https://doi.org/10.1017/pan.2025.5" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Read →</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="carousel-item">
        <div class="featured-paper">
          <h3>Inference at the data's edge: Gaussian processes for extrapolation uncertainty</h3>
          <p class="lead">Uses Gaussian-process regression to make model-dependency visible — quantifying rather than masking the uncertainty that arises when we extrapolate beyond well-supported regions of covariate space.</p>
          <p class="meta">Cho, Kim &amp; Hazlett · <em>Political Analysis</em> · 2026</p>
          <a href="https://doi.org/10.1017/pan.2026.10032" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-primary">Read →</a>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <button class="carousel-control-prev" type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide="prev">
      <span class="carousel-control-prev-icon" aria-hidden="true"></span>
      <span class="visually-hidden">Previous</span>
    </button>
    <button class="carousel-control-next" type="button" data-bs-target="#featured-carousel" data-bs-slide="next">
      <span class="carousel-control-next-icon" aria-hidden="true"></span>
      <span class="visually-hidden">Next</span>
    </button>
  </div>
</section>

<nav class="home-blocks" aria-label="Site sections">
  <a class="block" href="research.html">
    <h3>Research</h3>
    <p>Identification strategies, sensitivity analysis, and flexible estimation — applied to civil violence, environment, and medicine.</p>
    <span class="more">More →</span>
  </a>
  <a class="block" href="teaching.html">
    <h3>Teaching</h3>
    <p>PS 200C (Political Science) and Stat 256 (Statistics) — graduate causal inference at UCLA.</p>
    <span class="more">More →</span>
  </a>
  <a class="block" href="software.html">
    <h3>Software</h3>
    <p>R packages: <code>sensemakr</code>, <code>KBAL</code> / <code>kpop</code>, <code>scqe</code>, <code>KRLS</code>, and more.</p>
    <span class="more">More →</span>
  </a>
  <a class="block" href="https://practicallycausal.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    <h3>Practical Causal Inference lab</h3>
    <p>A research and training hub for applied causal inference, co-directed with Onyebuchi Arah.</p>
    <span class="more">Visit →</span>
  </a>
</nav>
```
